hero.headline: A new worksheet every day. Never the same one twice.
hero.subline: Kalmo Kids makes printable, personalized worksheets and screen-free sessions built around calm and focus — a fresh one for every child, in seconds.

step1.title: Profile
step1.body: Tell us your child's age and interests — 30 seconds.

step2.title: Daily plan
step2.body: Pick a development goal, theme and duration; we assemble the session.

step3.title: Print
step3.body: Print the worksheet and answer key — ready for a screen-free session.

gallery.title: Six worksheet types, endless variation
gallery.body: Every worksheet is algorithmically generated and unique — it never repeats.

pitch.title: 30 minutes of movement, memory games and creativity
pitch.body: Every daily plan mixes movement, memory and creative tasks around the worksheets — your child grows without ever needing a screen.

family.name: Family
family.body: Premium for multiple children, one subscription

school.name: School
school.body: For institutions, custom terms — get in touch

auth.title: Log in
auth.subtitle: Sign in to start today's session.

auth.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
auth.checkEmailLocalHint: In local development the email lands in the Mailpit test inbox, not your real one — open <url>http://localhost:54324</url>

onboarding.title: Let's meet your child
onboarding.subtitle: This is what we'll base the daily worksheets on.
onboarding.editSubtitle: Update your child's profile — the daily worksheets follow these.
onboarding.birthDateError: Choose a birth month in the past — the child must be 10 or younger.

accessibility.lowInkDesc: Less filled area — easier on the printer

childLimit.title: Upgrade to add another child
childLimit.body: The Free and Premium plans cover one child. Family covers up to 4 children on a single subscription — and your current child keeps everything.

collectionSheet.error: Couldn't prepare it. Please try again.
collectionSheet.offer: Collect the week's wins — print a fresh sheet to color in.

pack.title: Weekly pack
pack.subtitle: Print several days at once — ready to run screen-free, even without a printer nearby.
pack.fixedNote: Difficulty is fixed at today's level; your feedback shapes the next pack.
pack.error: Couldn't build the pack. Please try again.
pack.rateLimited: Just a moment — you've generated a lot very quickly. Try again shortly.
pack.gatedTitle: This pack is bigger than your free week
pack.gatedBody: It has more worksheets than your free weekly allowance has left. Pick fewer days, or upgrade for unlimited worksheets.
pack.worksheetNote: The worksheet is on the next page.
pack.wizardFooter: Planning several days ahead? Make a weekly pack.

wizard.title: Build today's session
wizard.subtitle: Pick what today's worksheet and plan should focus on.
wizard.difficultyManualHint: Defaults to the child's age — adjust if needed.
wizard.errorGeneric: Couldn't start the session. Please try again.

difficultyHint.4: Challenging — for a child who's ready.

wizard.difficultyAutoHint: We set the level per development goal based on how past sessions went. Turn it off to choose the level yourself.
wizard.rateLimited: Just a moment — you're starting sessions very fast. Try again in a minute.

print.answerKeyNote: The answer key is for you, the parent.
print.dailyPlanIntro: Every step in pictures — print it and you won't need a screen for the session.
print.dailyPlanWorksheetNote: Print this as its own worksheet.

settings.adaptiveHint: When on, the level follows your child by itself: it rises slowly while things go well, and eases off straight away after a hard session. When off, you set the level for every session.

history.title: History
history.emptyBody: Completed sessions will show up here.

sessionView.errorGeneric: Couldn't save feedback. Please try again.
sessionView.easeHint: This is how we know what to bring next time.

goalDescriptions.attention: How long they can stay on one task and tune out distractions.
goalDescriptions.working_memory: Holding information in mind while using it — like remembering what they saw while copying it.
goalDescriptions.executive_function: Planning, following rules and inhibiting impulses — the "stop, think, then act" skill.
goalDescriptions.visual_perception: Recognizing shapes, patterns and spatial relationships — the visual foundation of reading and writing.
goalDescriptions.bilateral_coordination: Both body sides (and brain halves) working together — one hand holds while the other works.
goalDescriptions.fine_motor: Small, precise hand and finger movements — from pencil grip to tying shoes.
goalDescriptions.pre_writing: The line control and pencil skills writing is built on, before any letters.
goalDescriptions.pre_reading: Letter and shape recognition, left-to-right tracking — the doorstep of reading.
goalDescriptions.math_thinking: Counting, sequences and spotting logical rules, in playful form.
goalDescriptions.creativity: Inventing and realizing their own ideas — open-ended, "no wrong answers" tasks.
goalDescriptions.problem_solving: Persistence and strategy when the solution is not immediately visible.

generatorDescriptions.maze: Your child finds the path from the dot to the star. They must plan ahead and follow corridors — training planning, visual scanning and pencil control.
generatorDescriptions.tracing: Tracing dashed lines starting at the green dot. The classic pre-writing drill: waves, zigzags and loops build the strokes letters are made of.
generatorDescriptions.pattern_completion: One element of each repeating pattern is missing — your child draws what comes next. Rule-spotting is the root of early math thinking.
generatorDescriptions.mirror_drawing: Half a figure is drawn on the left; your child draws its mirror image across the dashed axis. Builds hemispheric cooperation and spatial reasoning.
generatorDescriptions.grid_copy: Symbols in the top grid must be copied to the exact same cells below. Trains working memory and attention — like copying from the classroom board.
generatorDescriptions.arrow_board: A wall sheet full of arrows. Your child calls out each direction fast — or, as a twist, the opposite. A reaction and inhibition drill.
generatorDescriptions.connect_the_dots: Joining numbered dots from 1 reveals a shape. Number order, counting and line control in one.
generatorDescriptions.counting: Count the objects in each row, write the number in the box. The core drill of one-to-one correspondence and numeral writing.
generatorDescriptions.matching: Draw a line from each outline shape to its solid shadow. Trains form constancy — harder levels add a tricky shadow with no partner.
generatorDescriptions.symmetry_grid: Colored cells on the left half of a grid; your child colors their mirror image across the axis. The grid-based little sibling of mirror drawing, from age 3–4.
generatorDescriptions.visual_search: The box shows a target shape; your child finds and circles all of them in a sea of look-alikes. Attention and scanning — the doorstep of letter recognition.
generatorDescriptions.dual_path: A marker in each hand: following the color sequence in the header, your child connects the dots in the left and right fields simultaneously. Intense hemispheric teamwork — a procedurally generated take on the popular "here-there" method.
generatorDescriptions.dual_find: The left hand circles the shape, the right hand circles its number — at the same time. Divided attention, visual scanning and bilateral coordination, with fresh grids on every sheet.
generatorDescriptions.memory_cards: Cut-out cards with matching picture pairs. The classic memory game builds working memory and visual recall — flip two, find the twins.
generatorDescriptions.logic_grid: A picture Sudoku: every row and column needs each shape once. Trains logical elimination and working memory — every puzzle has exactly one solution.
generatorDescriptions.color_by_rule: A stained-glass panel where each area is numbered. The child colours by the legend — attention, one-to-one rule following and steady colouring.
generatorDescriptions.sequencing: Picture stories shown out of order — a flower growing, a glass filling. The child numbers each step, training cause-and-effect and ordering.
generatorDescriptions.cut_and_paste: A pattern with gaps and cut-out pieces at the bottom. The child cuts and pastes each piece into place — pattern logic plus scissor skills.
generatorDescriptions.hidden_objects: A tangle of overlapping outlines hides target shapes. The child finds, circles and counts each one — focused attention and figure-ground scanning.
generatorDescriptions.reward_chart: Not a task — a keepsake. A page of shapes — a tree, a flower, a balloon bunch, a fish or a caterpillar — your child colors or stickers, one for every finished task or day. A whole week of effort, made visible.

warmup.simon_says: Face your child and give commands: "Simon says: touch your nose!" They only act when "Simon says" is included — if they move without it, start over. 5-6 rounds warm up nicely.
warmup.finger_gym: Sit facing each other. Demonstrate: touch your thumb to each finger in turn, slow then faster, then both hands at once. Your child mirrors you.
warmup.rhythm_copy: Clap a short rhythm (clap-clap-pause-clap); your child claps it back. Add one beat each round. Once easy, switch claps for knee taps or stomps.
warmup.breathing_balloon: Sit comfortably. Put your hands on your bellies and slowly “inflate the balloon” as you breathe in, then let the air out. Repeat five times, slower each round.
warmup.mirror_me: Stand facing your child. Move very slowly — raise a hand, lean to one side — and your child mirrors you. Then swap: they lead and you mirror them.
warmup.wake_up_stretch: Stretch up tall like a tree, then curl up small like a seed. Shake out your hands and feet, then take one big breath. Now the body is ready to focus.
warmup.name_clap: Clap out the syllables of your names: Mi-a, two claps. Then try other words — animals, foods. Count how many claps each one takes.

movement.cross_crawl: Standing, your child touches right elbow to left knee, then the reverse, slowly alternating. 10-15 reps. Crossing the midline stimulates the two brain halves to cooperate.
movement.ball_target: Set a target (basket, pot, cushion) a few steps away. Your child throws a ball at it — after each hit, one step further back. Harder: non-dominant hand or underarm throw.
movement.obstacle_course: Tape lines on the floor: straight, zigzag, spiral. Your child walks them on tiptoe, on heels, sideways or hopping — you call which way per segment.
movement.animal_walks: Call out animals; your child moves like them: bear walk (all fours, knees up), crab walk (belly up), frog jumps, stork steps. 3-4 meters per segment, 2-3 rounds.
movement.balloon_keep_up: Blow up a balloon (or use a soft ball). Your child taps it to keep it off the floor. Make it harder: only palms, only head, or sitting down.
movement.hopscotch: Tape a hopscotch grid on the floor (one foot, two feet, one foot). Your child hops down and back. Count the squares out loud together.
movement.bean_bag_balance: Put a small soft object (a rolled sock, a cushion) on your child's head. They walk slowly across the room without it falling. Make it harder: on tiptoe, or winding.
movement.freeze_dance: Clap or hum a beat — your child dances. When you stop, they freeze like a statue. If they move, share a laugh and carry on. Swap: let them stop you.

memory.cup_shuffle: Hide a small object under one of three upturned cups. Slowly swap the cups while your child watches, then they point to the object. After a hit, speed up or add a fourth cup.
memory.whats_missing: Lay out 4-6 small objects; your child studies them for 10 seconds. Cover them, remove one, reveal: what is missing? Add objects to raise difficulty.
memory.sound_sequence: Eyes closed, your child listens as you make 2-3 sounds in a row (clap, jingling keys, knocking). They name the sounds and their order. Extend the sequence gradually.
memory.what_changed: Sit facing your child. While their eyes are closed, change one thing about yourself — roll up a sleeve, take off a hat. Eyes open: what changed? Then swap.
memory.story_chain: Start: “I went to the shop and bought an apple.” Your child repeats it and adds one thing. Take turns growing the list, trying to recall the whole chain each time.
memory.touch_and_tell: Put 4-5 small objects in a bag or sock. Your child reaches in, feels one, and names it before pulling it out. Afterwards, see if they can recall everything inside.
memory.copy_the_tower: Build a small tower of blocks (3-5 pieces). Your child studies it for ten seconds, then cover it. They build the same one from memory. Compare them together.

creative.build_story: Build a scene together from blocks (castle, farm, space station) while your child narrates what happens there. You only ask: "Who lives here? What happened yesterday?"
creative.draw_theme: Let them draw freely on today’s theme ({theme}). No corrections — at the end, ask them to tell you about the drawing.
creative.cut_collage: Your child cuts shapes from old magazines or colored paper and glues them into a picture. The cutting itself is fine-motor work — theme it to today's session.
creative.shadow_shapes: Shine a lamp or torch on the wall. Make shadow shapes with your hands: a dog, a bird, a rabbit. Guess each other's, then make up a little story about them.
creative.paper_fold: Fold a sheet of paper into something: a plane, a boat, a hat. It doesn't need to be perfect — careful creases are the point. Then test it out together.
creative.object_faces: Gather a few small things (buttons, pebbles, pasta, leaves). Arrange them into a face or an animal on the table. Ask who it is and what kind of mood they're in.
creative.junk_build: Set out a few clean bits of recycling (a box, caps, a cardboard tube). Your child builds something from them — a vehicle, a robot, a castle. Don't steer it; just ask about it at the end.

reward.sticker_moment: At the end, your child picks a sticker and places it on their collection sheet or finished worksheet. Let them decide where.
reward.victory_dance: One minute of free celebration dancing to their favorite song — or you clap the beat. What matters is celebrating together.
reward.high_five_tower: Give each other a run of high-fives: up high, down low, to the side, both hands, slow then lightning fast. Count how many you land without a miss.
reward.proud_moment: Ask your child to show today's work to someone — a sibling, a grandparent, even over a call. In one sentence, they say what they did and what they're proudest of.
reward.choose_the_song: Your child picks a favourite song and you celebrate to it together: dancing, clapping, or just nodding along. One song is enough — it's their reward for today's work.
reward.cheer_chant: Make up a short cheer together using your child's name: “Mi-a, hooray, you rocked today!” Say it out loud with claps. You can end future sessions the same way.
reward.reward_stamp: Draw a little star or stamp on the back of your child's hand or on their collection sheet. Say out loud why they earned it — one specific thing they did well today.

reflection.favorite_part: Ask: "What was your favorite part today? What was hardest?" Listen without judging — the goal is putting the experience into words.
reflection.show_and_tell: Your child shows the finished sheet to someone (other parent, sibling, grandparent on a call) and explains what they did. Pride locks in the learning.
reflection.thumbs_check: Ask how today felt and have them answer with a thumb: up (good), sideways (okay), down (hard). There's no wrong answer — whatever they show, gently ask why.
reflection.one_word: Ask your child to sum up today in a single word — anything: “funny”, “tricky”, “good”. You say one too. No need to explain it, but you can listen if they want to.
reflection.hardest_easiest: Ask: what was the easiest thing today, and what was the hardest? Accept whatever they say — the point isn't performance, it's helping them notice how things went.
reflection.teach_me: Ask your child to teach you one thing they did today — a shape, a move, a rule. When they're the “teacher”, they understand and remember it better.
reflection.tomorrow_wish: Ask what they'd like to try or do again next time. It's not a promise, just a wish — but it shows you what they're drawn to and looking forward to.

deleteChild.title: Delete child
deleteChild.description: Deleting this child permanently removes their profile and every session, worksheet, and piece of progress. This cannot be undone.

welcome.subject: Welcome to Kalmo Kids! 🎉
welcome.body:
Hi! {childName}'s profile is ready. From now on you can make a fresh, personalized worksheet and a screen-free session together every day. Have fun!

— The Kalmo Kids team

deletion.subject: Confirm your account deletion
deletion.heading: Delete your Kalmo Kids account?
deletion.body: You asked to delete your Kalmo Kids account. This permanently removes everything — your children's profiles, every session, worksheet and piece of progress — and cannot be undone. To go ahead, confirm on the next page:
deletion.ignore: If you didn't request this, just ignore this email — nothing happens and your account stays exactly as it is.

legal.draftNotice: This is a good-faith draft, pending legal review. It is not final legal text.

deleteAccount.title: Delete account
deleteAccount.description: For safety, deleting your account is a two-step, email-confirmed process. Nothing is removed until you confirm from the link we email you.
deleteAccount.requestIntro: We'll email a confirmation link to {email}. Deleting is only final after you confirm on that page, and the link is valid for one hour.
deleteAccount.sent: Check your email — we sent a confirmation link to {email}. Nothing is deleted until you confirm there.
deleteAccount.devLinkNote: Email isn't configured in this environment. Open the confirmation page directly:
deleteAccount.requestError: Couldn't start deletion. Please try again.
deleteAccount.error: Couldn't delete. Check the email you typed and try again.

accountDelete.title: Confirm account deletion
accountDelete.intro: This permanently deletes your Kalmo Kids account and everything in it. It cannot be undone.
accountDelete.whatChildren: {count} child profile(s), with every session, worksheet and piece of progress
accountDelete.expiredTitle: This link has expired or is invalid
accountDelete.expiredBody: Deletion links last one hour and only work for the account that requested them. Start again from Settings.
